key_insight: AI tutors for high-stakes math should facilitate reasoning repair and verification, not just force Socratic dialogue or final-answer generation.
engineering_relevance: The paper translates pedagogical theory into concrete UI features—layered worked examples, step-linked visual grounding, and metacognitive scaffolding—that can reduce the cost of debugging student reasoning in real tutoring systems.
method: Generative design study of AITutor features
Usability study with junior-high exam-prep learners
evidence_signal: Evidence combines 7,379 telemetry events, 8 contextual observations, and 10 interviews showing students often prefer answer-first shortcuts as diagnostic checkpoints under time pressure.
watch_item: A key implementation concern is that in high-stakes contexts, overly rigid Socratic interaction may frustrate learners and add friction unless the system supports fast inspection and local repair.
paper_title: From Answer Generators to Reasoning Facilitators: Designing AI Tutors for Mathematical Reasoning in High-Stakes Environments
